description: Detector definitions used for scattering optical presets and alias-based auto-detect.

presets.name: Apogee - Forward
presets.description: Apogee forward detector modeled as the forward half of a large phi=45 degree collection lens.

presets.name: Apogee - Side
presets.description: Apogee side detector modeled as the side half of a large phi=45 degree collection lens.

presets.name: BD FACSCanto II FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for BD FACSCanto II style modeling. Public documentation describes FSC as a photodiode with 488/10 BP filtering; the numerical aperture values here are modeling assumptions.

presets.name: BD FACSCanto II SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for BD FACSCanto II style modeling. Public documentation describes SSC as a PMT with 488/10 BP filtering; the numerical aperture values here are modeling assumptions.

presets.name: Beckman Coulter CytoFLEX Fluorescence
presets.description: Generic fluorescence detector preset for CytoFLEX style modeling. CytoFLEX systems are available with up to 3 lasers and 13 color research configurations; the numerical aperture values here are effective modeling assumptions.

presets.name: Beckman Coulter CytoFLEX SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for CytoFLEX style modeling. CytoFLEX instruments emphasize high sensitivity optical collection; the numerical aperture values here are effective modeling assumptions.

presets.name: Agilent NovoCyte FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for Agilent NovoCyte style modeling. The numerical aperture values here are effective modeling assumptions intended to give RosettaX a documented starting point rather than an instrument-certified optical reconstruction.

presets.name: Agilent NovoCyte SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for Agilent NovoCyte style modeling. The numerical aperture values here are effective modeling assumptions intended to support RosettaX detector selection and geometric calibration workflows.

presets.name: Cytek Aurora FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for Cytek Aurora style modeling. These values are effective geometry assumptions for RosettaX, not a claim that every Aurora configuration shares the same optics.

presets.name: Cytek Aurora SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for Cytek Aurora style modeling. These values are effective geometry assumptions for RosettaX, chosen to support consistent calibration workflows rather than exact vendor reconstruction.

presets.name: Sony ID7000 FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for Sony ID7000 style modeling. These parameters are packaged as effective RosettaX assumptions so the instrument family can be selected and documented consistently.

presets.name: Sony ID7000 SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for Sony ID7000 style modeling. These parameters are packaged as effective RosettaX assumptions so the instrument family can be selected and documented consistently.

presets.name: nanoFCM NanoAnalyzer FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for nanoFCM NanoAnalyzer style modeling. These values are effective RosettaX assumptions intended to support documented small-particle calibration workflows rather than reproduce one proprietary optical train exactly.

presets.name: nanoFCM NanoAnalyzer SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for nanoFCM NanoAnalyzer style modeling. These values are effective RosettaX assumptions intended to support documented small-particle calibration workflows rather than reproduce one proprietary optical train exactly.

presets.name: Thermo Fisher Attune NxT FSC
presets.description: Forward scatter detector preset for Thermo Fisher Attune NxT style modeling. These values are effective RosettaX modeling assumptions suitable for documenting and reproducing one chosen geometry in calibration work.

presets.name: Thermo Fisher Attune NxT SSC
presets.description: Side scatter detector preset for Thermo Fisher Attune NxT style modeling. These values are effective RosettaX modeling assumptions suitable for documenting and reproducing one chosen geometry in calibration work.
